conference.name: NEHA AEC 2026
conference.venue: Sheraton Kansas City Hotel at Crown Center

positioning: HS Cloud Suite is a modern, cloud-based platform purpose-built for Environmental Health (EH) and regulatory agencies. It centralizes inspections, permitting, licensing, workflows, payments, reporting, and citizen-facing services into one unified system.
HS Cloud Suite is comprised of 5 products:
HS Cloud: Centralized data management platform
HS Cloud Mobile: Offline virtual inspection app
HS Pay: Unified online payment processor

In short: HS Cloud Suite replaces outdated, disconnected systems with a single digital platform that streamlines regulatory operations, improves transparency, and increases efficiency for both agencies and the communities they serve.
Core Functions of the HS Cloud Suite

summary: In the context of the NEHA (National Environmental Health Association), CE stands for Continuing Education (often referred to as CE credits, CE units, or contact hours).

CEs are a mandatory requirement for environmental health professionals who hold NEHA credentials. They ensure that practitioners stay up-to-date with the latest science, regulations, trends, and technologies in the environmental health field.
To maintain and renew a professional NEHA credential, you must accumulate a specific number of continuing education contact hours during every two-year credential cycle.
Generally, 1 hour of learning equals 1.0 CE credit. If you do not meet the CE requirements by the end of your two-year period, your credential can lapse.
Different NEHA credentials require different amounts of CE hours every two years:
tips: How to Earn and Report CEs
How to Earn Them

You can earn CEs through a variety of professional development activities, provided they relate directly to environmental health (e.g., food safety, water quality, wastewater, air quality, pest control, or hazardous materials). Examples include:
Attending the NEHA Annual Educational Conference (AEC) & Exhibition.
Completing online training, webinars, and courses through NEHA’s E-Learning platform.
Attending approved third-party conferences, seminars, university classes, or lectures.
How to Report Them
Professionals log into their MyNEHA online account to self-report their credits.
Self-Reporting: You enter the course name, date, and the number of hours earned. NEHA typically reviews and verifies these hours within a week.
Automatic Tracking: For certain official events, like the NEHA AEC, the association will automatically apply the CE credits to your account after you attend.
Tip: Always keep your certificates of completion and course documentation for your own records. NEHA conducts random audits, and you will need proof of your hours if you are selected.

summary: A Comprehensive Guide to the Sheraton Kansas City Hotel at Crown Center

If you are planning a stay at the Sheraton Kansas City Hotel at Crown Center, you are looking at one of the most centrally located, iconic high-rise hotels in the city. Rising 45 stories above the Crossroads Arts District and Crown Center, it offers fantastic views and unbeatable convenience—especially if you want to explore without dealing with the elements.

Before diving into the rooms, the absolute best feature of this hotel is The Link. It is a climate-controlled, elevated glass walkway that connects the Sheraton directly to the Crown Center Shops and Union Station. You can walk to restaurants, shopping, theaters, and attractions without ever having to step outside, which is a massive plus during chilly winter months or humid summer days.
You won't have to wander far for a meal, as the hotel features a few distinct onsite options:

Spectators (Signature Restaurant & Pub): This is the main hub for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. It has a casual, sports-pub atmosphere serving American classics and authentic Kansas City barbecue (including their signature Cows & Hogs Are Friends BBQ platters). They also feature a great selection of local craft beers.
The Link Café: Perfect for the morning rush. Located near the walkway entrance, it serves Starbucks coffee, pastries, and quick grab-and-go breakfast or lunch items.
tips: In-Room Dining: Full all-day menus (including the BBQ items from Spectators) can be delivered straight to your room.
The American: Historically an iconic standalone restaurant, this architecturally stunning space with floor-to-ceiling windows is now used as the hotel’s premium private event and banquet venue.
🏊‍♂️ Key Hotel Amenities
Heated Rooftop Pool: An outdoor, heated saltwater pool located on a rooftop deck that offers great views of the downtown skyline.
Sheraton Fitness Center: A fully equipped, 24/7 gym with modern cardio machines, free weights, and life-fitness equipment.

Sheraton Club Lounge: If you book a Club-level room or hold Marriott Platinum status (or higher), you get access to the Club Lounge. It's located at the top of the hotel (in the space of the former revolving Skies restaurant), offering panoramic, birds-eye views of KC alongside complimentary breakfast, evening hors d'oeuvres, and drinks.
Pet-Friendly: You can bring your dog along (though weight limits and a standard pet fee apply).

Parking: Both self-parking and valet parking are available in an attached garage. Insider tip: Like most downtown convention hotels, parking fees can add up daily, so factor that into your travel budget.
🛏️ Rooms & "Insider Details"
The hotel boasts over 700 rooms and suites.
The Views: When booking, try to request a high-floor room facing west or north for spectacular views of the downtown skyline and the Crown Center fountains.

The Microwave Situation: Standard rooms do not come with microwaves. Instead, the hotel places communal microwaves on the even-numbered floors near the ice machines. If having a microwave in-room is a dealbreaker, you'll want to request one from front desk staff early or look into booking a larger suite.

Layout: Because it is a massive convention hotel with over 100,000 square feet of meeting space, the lobby and hallways can occasionally feel like a labyrinth during major events. Just follow the signs for the elevators!
📍 Connected & Nearby Attractions
Because of its stellar location, you are steps away from a huge chunk of Kansas City's best tourist spots:
hotel.source: A Comprehensive Guide to the Sheraton Kansas City Hotel at Crown Center

nearby.walk: Union Station, 30 W Pershing Rd — 7-minute walk via The Link

nearby.walk: 2980 McGee Trafficway — 10-minute walk

nearby.walk: Crown Center Shops, Level 1 — 4-minute walk via The Link

nearby.walk: The Westin, 1 E Pershing Rd — 5-minute walk via The Link

nearby.walk: 2460 Pershing Road, Lobby Level — 5-minute walk

nearby.name: Jack Stack Barbecue - Freight House
nearby.meta: 101 W 22nd St — 12-minute walk
nearby.walk: 101 W 22nd St — 12-minute walk
nearby.description: The definitive upscale barbecue destination in KC. Housed in a historic 110-year-old brick freight building with 25-foot ceilings, white-tablecloth service, legendary burnt ends, lamb ribs, and hickory-pit baked beans.

nearby.name: Burnt End BBQ
nearby.meta: Crown Center Shops, Level 1 — 4-minute walk via The Link
nearby.walk: Crown Center Shops, Level 1 — 4-minute walk via The Link
nearby.description: A championship-winning fast-casual barbecue outlet. Specializes in custom build-your-own BBQ bowls, loaded brisket mac & cheese, and competition smoked platters.

nearby.name: Novel KC
nearby.meta: 1927 McGee St — 5-minute walk
nearby.walk: 1927 McGee St — 5-minute walk
nearby.description: A magnificent, critically acclaimed hidden gem blocks north of your hotel. Chef Ryan Brazeal offers high-concept contemporary fine dining (Hamachi crudo, seared diver scallops, handmade duck agnolotti) across from a jaw-dropping 50-foot glass tile mosaic and open kitchen.

nearby.walk: 30 W Pershing Rd — 7-minute walk via The Link

nearby.name: Lidia’s Kansas City
nearby.meta: 101 W 22nd St — 11-minute walk
nearby.walk: 101 W 22nd St — 11-minute walk

nearby.walk: 101 W 22nd St — 10-minute walk

nearby.name: The Rockhill Grille

nearby.walk: 2000 Grand Blvd — 8-minute walk

nearby.walk: 30 W Pershing Rd — 7-minute walk via The Link
